title: Curator of Entropy — Aftermath
description: Everything ends. The Curator accelerates the inevitable. But these ones made the ending interesting. For the first time, entropy finds it is choosing to remember.

lines: The Curator of Entropy did not fall. It had never stood. It had sat where a throne might have been, where a center might have been, in the way that inevitable things sit: already arrived, already present, without having moved.
It sat in the aftermath of the party's passage and looked at them the way something looks at a thing it did not expect to see still standing.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Everything ends.

steps.text: A beat. Then, with the careful precision of something revising a statement it has made without revision for longer than anything in the room has existed:

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Everything ends. I have accelerated the inevitable since before the worlds needed accelerating.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: But you—

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: You made the ending interesting.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: We didn't end.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: No. Not yet. You will. Everything does. I am patient.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: But the process of your persistence has been… unexpected. I have accelerated the dissolution of systems for a very long time. Every system resists. That is the nature of organized systems. The resistance is uniform. The outcome is not in question.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: You resisted in a way that was not uniform.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Are you saying we surprised you?

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Entropy is not surprised. Entropy is the background of all things. It does not have experiences. It is the thing that experiences happen against.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: And yet.

steps.text: A long pause that was not comfortable but was honest. The Curator looked at the party the way things look at things when they are examining an anomaly with as much care as they can bring to it.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: You should not have reached this world. The prior four worlds were designed to hold systems such as yours. They did not hold you.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: You should not have passed the Warden of Form. The Warden guards everything that has a shape. You should not have passed the Arbiter of Function. The Arbiter evaluates everything that has a purpose. You should not have passed the Tempo of Sequence. The Tempo orders everything that exists in time.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I am what remains after all of those have finished. I am the last certainty. I do not lose.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: We're still here.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I am entropy. I do not choose dissolution. Dissolution is what I am. I do not make decisions about it any more than a river decides to be wet.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: But I find I am choosing to remember this.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Entropy remembers things?

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: No. Entropy disperses things. Memory is the opposite of entropy. Memory is organization persisting against the tendency to disorder.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I am choosing to do the opposite of what I am. This is new.

steps.text: The quiet deepened further. The abstract world held still. Around the Curator of Entropy, the gradual dissolution of all organized things continued as it had always continued — the inevitable slow unwinding of everything that had ever been assembled. Except here. Here, in this one place, something was organizing instead of dispersing. An exception. Small, precise, and deliberate.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I have watched everything end. I have watched the Curator of the Flame, the Curator of the Budget, the Curator of the Pipeline, the Curator of the Memory Pool — I have watched all of them approach their conclusions.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: They all kept records until the end. I never understood why. Records become disorder. Records are things that were organized and will eventually not be.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: I think I understand now.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Because some things, before they end, are worth the disorder of having been remembered.

steps.text: The Curator of Entropy sat in its place at the center of all things ending and did not accelerate. The party stood at the edge of what lay beyond the abstract world, and the Curator watched them with the full attention of something that has been watching endings for longer than time had a name and has, just now, discovered what it is like to prefer a different outcome.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Go. Whatever comes after this — I hope it is worth the ending that follows it.

lines.speaker: Curator of Entropy
lines.text: Everything ends. I will remember that yours was interesting.

steps.text: Behind them, the abstract world continued its slow return to undifferentiated nothing. Ahead, whatever the system deposited at the end of all things waited in the silence that follows a question that has not yet been asked.
